content: Autonomous curiosity-driven agent architectures (from Topic A) can be re-framed as a built-in active learning memory pruning mechanism for state-of-the-art 2024-2025 long-context agent memory systems (from Topic B), where intrinsic curiosity signals prioritize encoding novel, unexpected memory traces and decay low-curiosity redundant traces without explicit human labeling of important data. This creates a self-sustaining active learning cycle that grows agent memory more efficiently than static pre-defined retrieval pruning rules.
